league.name: Major League Soccer
league.description: MLS is the top men's league in the United States and Canada — playoffs, Designated Players, and growing North American rivalries.
league.styleOfPlay: Athletic and direct, with squad-building rules that mix homegrown players, MLS veterans, and star imports.
famousClubs: Seattle Sounders FC — strong fan culture
famousPlayers: Landon Donovan — LA Galaxy era icon
league.historySummary: Founded in 1996, MLS expanded across the US and Canada and now feeds CONCACAF Champions Cup and World Cup storylines.
rivalries: LAFC vs LA Galaxy — El Tráfico
league.fanGuide: Learn playoff format first, then pick a city rivalry. Club soccer here is as much about community and stadium atmosphere as European tables.

teams.name: Atlanta United FC
teams.shortHistory: Atlanta United FC is in the FootyBrain controlled expansion club set.
teams.fanGuide: Learn Atlanta United FC's colours and main rivalries as editorial coverage expands.

teams.name: Inter Miami CF
teams.shortHistory: Inter Miami CF turned global attention toward MLS with Designated Player signings and a pink-shirt brand built in South Florida.
teams.fanGuide: Chase Stadium nights mix celebrity storylines with improving squad depth — learn Messi-era context, then the Florida derby with Orlando.

teams.name: LA Galaxy
teams.shortHistory: LA Galaxy are MLS royalty — five Cups, Hollywood appeal, and the league's original star-import blueprint.
teams.fanGuide: Carson-era history matters: Beckham, Donovan, and now a veteran mix with Marco Reus — start with El Tráfico vs LAFC.

teams.name: Los Angeles FC
teams.shortHistory: LAFC arrived as a sleek Los Angeles expansion project and quickly became an MLS trophy contender with strong South American talent.
teams.fanGuide: BMO Stadium atmosphere and the El Tráfico rivalry with LA Galaxy are the two names every new fan should know first.

teams.name: San Diego FC

teams.fanGuide: Learn San Diego FC's colours and main rivalries as editorial coverage expands.

teams.name: Seattle Sounders FC
teams.shortHistory: Seattle Sounders FC turned Pacific Northwest passion into MLS trophies and the loudest recurring atmospheres in the league.
teams.fanGuide: Learn the Cascadia Cup with Portland first — Lumen Field noise and green scarves are the culture before you memorize the roster.

players.name: Ajani Fortune
players.quickFact: Trinidad and Tobago midfielder and Atlanta United homegrown talent who covers ground in central midfield.
players.playingStyle: Energetic central midfielder who presses, carries the ball, and supports both phases with late runs.
quizHints: Trinidad and Tobago international in Atlanta United red and black.
Central midfielder who presses and links defence to attack.
Atlanta homegrown — not Jamaica or USMNT by default in hints.

players.name: Aleksey Miranchuk
players.quickFact: Russia attacking midfielder with Champions League experience who became a creative outlet for Atlanta United.
players.playingStyle: Technical number 10 who drifts between lines, combines in tight spaces, and shoots from distance.
quizHints: Russia international playmaker who joined MLS from European football.
Attacking midfielder for Atlanta United, not Seattle Sounders.
Left-footed creator in red and black for Atlanta.

players.name: Matías Galarza
players.quickFact: Paraguay midfielder who shields Atlanta United’s back line and distributes with composure from deep.
players.playingStyle: Disciplined midfielder who breaks up play, shields defenders, and recycles possession with short passes.
quizHints: Paraguay international who plays central midfield for Atlanta United.
Defensive-minded midfielder in red and black, not a winger.
South American anchor for Atlanta — not Aleksey Miranchuk the creator.

players.name: Miguel Almirón
players.quickFact: Paraguay winger whose pace and direct running made him an Atlanta United star before Premier League spells with Newcastle.
players.playingStyle: Direct winger who drives inside, shoots from the right channel, and presses aggressively.
quizHints: Paraguay winger in red, white, and blue with MLS and Premier League experience.
Atlanta United legend — not Gustavo Gómez the centre-back captain.
Miguel who attacks from the right with quick feet.

players.name: Saba Lobjanidze
players.quickFact: Georgia winger whose direct dribbling and end product made him one of Atlanta United’s most exciting wide threats.
players.playingStyle: Direct winger who takes on full-backs, cuts inside, and delivers dangerous balls into the box.
quizHints: Georgia international who attacks from wide areas for Atlanta United.
Left-footed winger in red and black known for dribbling and crosses.
Plays for Atlanta — not Seattle or LAFC.

players.name: Zack Steffen
players.quickFact: United States international goalkeeper in the linked USMNT national pool.
players.playingStyle: Shot-stopper who commands the penalty area and distributes with both feet.
quizHints: American goalkeeper linked to the United States.
Keeper in stars and stripes — not Dest the full-back.
Plays in MLS.

players.name: Héctor Herrera
players.quickFact: Mexico midfielder who captained Porto and Atlético Madrid before MLS and brings calm possession to El Tri’s midfield.
players.playingStyle: Deep-lying midfielder who circulates possession, switches play, and tackles selectively.
quizHints: Mexico midfielder in green with Porto and Atlético Madrid experience.
Veteran passer — not Jonathan González the younger MLS midfielder.
Héctor who controls tempo from central midfield.

players.name: Lionel Messi
players.quickFact: Argentina’s 2022 World Cup-winning number 10 who moved to Inter Miami in 2023 and reshaped MLS attention overnight.
players.playingStyle: Left-footed creator who drifts inside, threads through-balls, and finishes with placement rather than power.
quizHints: Won the World Cup with Argentina in Qatar while wearing number 10.
Left European giants for pink-and-black Inter Miami after Paris Saint-Germain.
Left-footed Argentina creator famous for dribbling, free kicks, and playmaking.

players.name: Luis Suárez
players.quickFact: Uruguay’s all-time leading scorer and longtime Barcelona partner of Messi who reunited with him at Inter Miami.
players.playingStyle: Streetwise centre-forward who finds half-yards, links play, and finishes first-time in the box.
quizHints: Uruguay number 9 known for elite movement and finishing in the box.
Shared a famous attacking partnership with Messi at Barcelona.
Veteran centre-forward in Inter Miami pink and black.

players.name: Rodrigo De Paul
players.quickFact: Argentina’s tireless World Cup-winning midfielder who drives Inter Miami’s press and progression from central midfield.
players.playingStyle: Box-to-box midfielder who carries the ball forward, presses aggressively, and plays line-breaking passes.
quizHints: Argentina midfielder who starred in Qatar 2022 beside Messi.
Known for relentless running and vertical passing from midfield.
Inter Miami engine in pink and black, not a winger.

players.name: Sergio Reguilón
players.quickFact: Spain left-back with Real Madrid and Premier League pedigree who overlaps for Inter Miami down the left flank.
players.playingStyle: Overlapping full-back who supports wide attacks, recovers with pace, and delivers driven crosses.
quizHints: Spain full-back developed at Real Madrid before Tottenham and Manchester United spells.
Attacking left-back who delivers crosses for Inter Miami.
European-trained defender in pink and black, not a centre-forward.

players.name: Brady Scott
players.quickFact: United States international goalkeeper in the linked USMNT national squad.
players.playingStyle: Agile goalkeeper who reacts quickly to close-range efforts.
quizHints: American goalkeeper linked to the United States.
Goalkeeper — not Steffen if both listed; younger MLS keeper.
Host nation squad member.

players.name: João Klauss
players.quickFact: Brazilian centre-forward whose hold-up play and box movement give LA Galaxy a physical focal point up front.
players.playingStyle: Target-style forward who links play with his back to goal and finishes close-range chances.
quizHints: Brazilian striker who leads the line in LA Galaxy white and gold.
Tall centre-forward known for hold-up play and aerial duels.
Plays up front for Galaxy — not Spain midfielder Riqui Puig.

players.name: Marco Reus
players.quickFact: Germany forward and one-club Borussia Dortmund legend who brought late-career creativity to LA Galaxy in white and gold.
players.playingStyle: Intelligent forward who arrives late in the box, combines quickly, and shoots with his left foot.
quizHints: Long-time Dortmund captain and Germany international before MLS.
Left-footed attacker for LA Galaxy in white and gold.
German playmaker — not Spain’s Riqui Puig on the same team.

players.name: Maya Yoshida
players.quickFact: Japan’s longtime captain and World Cup centre-back who brought Serie A and Premier League experience to LA Galaxy.
players.playingStyle: Composed centre-back who reads the game, wins headers, and builds play calmly from the back.
quizHints: Former Japan captain who played for Southampton and Sampdoria.
Experienced centre-back in LA Galaxy white and gold.
Japanese international defender — not Marco Reus in attack.

players.name: Riqui Puig
players.quickFact: Spain midfielder from Barcelona’s La Masia who became LA Galaxy’s tempo-setter after leaving Camp Nou.
players.playingStyle: Technical midfielder who keeps the ball, plays between lines, and dictates tempo in possession.
quizHints: La Masia graduate who left Barcelona for MLS with LA Galaxy.
Small Spain midfielder known for tight control and passing rhythm.
Central playmaker in white and gold — not Germany’s Marco Reus.

players.name: Aaron Long
players.quickFact: United States centre-back and former MLS Defender of the Year who anchors LAFC’s back line after the New York Red Bulls.
players.playingStyle: Aggressive centre-back who wins aerial duels, steps into passes, and leads the defensive line vocally.
quizHints: USMNT centre-back who won MLS Defender of the Year with the Red Bulls.
Physical American defender in black and gold for LAFC.
Organises the back line — not LA Galaxy white and gold.

players.name: Denis Bouanga
players.quickFact: Gabon international whose pace and direct runs made him one of MLS’s most dangerous wide forwards with LAFC.
players.playingStyle: Direct wide forward who drives at defenders, cuts inside, and finishes with power or placement.
quizHints: Gabon winger who attacks from the left for black-and-gold LAFC.
MLS forward known for speed, dribbling, and cutting inside to shoot.
Not a centre-back — plays wide for Los Angeles FC.

players.name: Heung-min Son
players.quickFact: South Korea captain whose goals at Tottenham made him Asia’s most recognisable Premier League star before a move to MLS with Los Angeles FC.
players.playingStyle: Inside forward who attacks space behind the defence, combines quickly, and finishes with both feet.
quizHints: South Korea captain in red who plays for Los Angeles FC in MLS.
Left-footed forward — not Kang-in Lee the creative midfielder.
Heung-min Son known for cut-ins from the left to shoot.

players.name: Ryan Porteous
players.quickFact: Scotland centre-back and 2024 European Championship squad member who brought Premier League physicality to LAFC.
players.playingStyle: Dominant centre-back who wins headers, blocks shots, and steps out to intercept through balls.
quizHints: Scotland defender who played for Watford and Hibernian before MLS.
Tall centre-back in black and gold for Los Angeles FC.
British international — not the USMNT centre-back Aaron Long.

players.name: Tyler Boyd
players.quickFact: United States winger with Japan roots who provides width and pressing energy on the left for LAFC.
players.playingStyle: Hard-working winger who stretches play, combines in wide areas, and presses with sustained energy.
quizHints: USMNT winger who can play on either flank for Los Angeles FC.
American wide player in black and gold after spells in Europe and Portugal.
Works the left channel for LAFC, not Seattle Rave Green.

players.name: James Rodríguez
players.quickFact: Colombia playmaker who won the Golden Boot at the 2014 World Cup and later starred in Europe before an MLS chapter with Minnesota United.
players.playingStyle: Classic attacking midfielder who finds space between lines, shoots from distance, and delivers set-piece quality.
quizHints: Colombia no. 10 in yellow who scored six goals at Brazil 2014.
Left-footed playmaker — not Cucho Hernández the striker.
James Rodríguez known for long-range shots and whipped crosses.

players.name: Emil Forsberg
players.quickFact: Sweden winger who starred for RB Leipzig in the Bundesliga before MLS with New York Red Bulls and long service for the Blågult.
players.playingStyle: Left-sided attacker who drifts inside, strikes from distance, and delivers quality from dead balls.
quizHints: Swedish left winger linked to Sweden’s national team.
RB Leipzig and NY Red Bulls attacker — not Alexander Isak the striker.
Emil who takes set pieces and cuts inside from the left.

players.name: Felipe Mora
players.quickFact: Chile striker who scored in Liga MX and MLS with Portland Timbers before becoming a squad forward option for La Roja.
players.playingStyle: Centre-forward who finds space in the box, links lay-offs, and finishes close-range chances.
quizHints: Chile forward in red who played for Portland Timbers in MLS.
Striker — not Erick Pulgar the defensive midfielder.
Felipe who attacks the box with movement off the shoulder.

players.name: Hirving Lozano
players.quickFact: Mexico winger whose pace at Pachuca and PSV earned a Napoli move; “Chucky” remains a direct outlet for El Tri on the left.
players.playingStyle: Explosive winger who drives at full-backs, cuts inside, and combines quick feet with direct running.
quizHints: Mexico winger in green known as Chucky with Napoli pedigree.
Fast left-sided attacker — not Hirving at PSV only; now San Diego FC in FootyBrain.
Hirving who cuts inside from the wing to shoot.

players.name: Albert Rusnák
players.quickFact: Slovakia attacking midfielder with Premier League experience who crafts chances as a number 10 for Seattle.
players.playingStyle: Skillful attacking midfielder who receives between lines, combines in the final third, and shoots from range.
quizHints: Slovakia international playmaker for Seattle Sounders in Rave Green.
Former Leeds and Real Salt Lake attacker before Seattle.
Creative number 10 — not USMNT winger Paul Arriola.

players.name: Cristian Roldán
players.quickFact: United States midfielder and Seattle Sounders cornerstone who has won multiple MLS Cups as a box-to-box engine.
players.playingStyle: Energetic midfielder who covers ground, presses, and links defence to attack with simple, quick passing.
quizHints: USMNT central midfielder in green for Seattle Sounders.
Box-to-box Sounders star — not his Salvadoran full-back brother Álex.
Seattle academy product who plays in midfield, not at right-back.

players.name: Jordan Morris
players.quickFact: United States forward and Seattle Sounders homegrown star known for pace, MLS Cup goals, and USMNT impact.
players.playingStyle: Pacey forward who attacks space in transition, presses aggressively, and finishes with composure.
quizHints: Seattle homegrown USMNT forward in Rave Green.
Fast American attacker who excels in transition and pressing.
Sounders forward — not the Salvadoran full-back Roldán on the same team.

players.name: Paul Arriola
players.quickFact: United States winger with Mexico club roots who brings experience and crossing from the right for Seattle.
players.playingStyle: Direct winger who takes on defenders, delivers crosses, and tracks back to help the full-back.
quizHints: USMNT winger who played for Tijuana before MLS and European spells.
Right-sided American attacker for Seattle Sounders.
Wide player in green — not Slovakia’s Albert Rusnák in the middle.

players.name: Sang-bin Jeong
players.quickFact: South Korea forward who moved from Wolverhampton to MLS with St. Louis City and offers pace off the bench for the national team.
players.playingStyle: Direct forward who attacks channels, presses defenders, and finishes on the break.
quizHints: South Korea attacker in red who plays for St. Louis City in MLS.
Young forward — not Heung-min Son at Tottenham.
Sang-bin who runs in behind with pace.

players.name: Benjamín Kuscevic
players.quickFact: Chile centre-back with Colo-Colo roots who moved through Europe to Toronto FC and defends solidly for La Roja.
players.playingStyle: No-nonsense centre-back who defends the box, clears danger, and plays simple passes out.
quizHints: Chile centre-back in red who plays for Toronto FC in MLS.
Colo-Colo graduate — not Gabriel Suazo the left-back.
Benjamín who wins headers and blocks shots centrally.

players.name: Andrés Cubas
players.quickFact: Paraguay defensive midfielder who anchors MLS sides Vancouver and previously Buenos Aires clubs with tough tackling for La Albirroja.
players.playingStyle: Compact defensive midfielder who tackles hard, protects centre-backs, and plays simple forward passes.
quizHints: Paraguay holding midfielder in red, white, and blue at Vancouver Whitecaps.
Deep no. 6 — not Matías Galarza the younger midfielder.
Andrés who shields the back line and wins second balls.
